hints.to wade: 'To wade' is not Latin 'vadere' — 'vadere' means to go or walk

hints.present indicative: Present indicative 2nd sg. would be 'vadis' — 'Vade' is imperative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'vadas' — 'Vade' is imperative
hints.future imperative: Future imperative would be 'vadito' — 'Vade' is present imperative

hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venire' (to come)
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the parts of 'videre' (to see)
hints.vivo, vivere, vixi, victum: These are the parts of 'vivere' (to live)

steps.prompt: Why is the pronoun 'tu' used with the imperative when it's already implied?
steps.target: emphatic: 'you too' / 'you yourself go and do likewise'
choices: emphatic: 'you too' / 'you yourself go and do likewise'

hints.Latin always requires subject pronouns: Latin is a pro-drop language — 'tu' is used for emphasis, not requirement
hints.it clarifies number (singular vs. plural: The imperative 'vade/fac' already show singular — 'tu' adds emphasis, not clarity
hints.it is a scribal error: 'Tu' is consistently attested — it is deliberate emphasis in Jesus's command

hints.to be: 'To be' is 'esse' — 'facere' means to do or make

steps.target: present imperative (2nd sg., irregular short form)
choices: present imperative (2nd sg., irregular short form)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'facis' — 'fac' is the shortened imperative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'facias' — 'fac' is imperative

hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the parts of 'ferre' (to carry)
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the parts of 'fugere' (to flee)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become) — passive of 'facere'

steps.prompt: 'Fac' is an irregular short imperative. Which other common verbs have this pattern?

hints.ama (love), mone (warn), audi (hear): These are regular imperatives — the irregular short forms are dic, duc, fac, fer
hints.i (go), es (be), da (give): 'I' and 'es' are irregular but for different reasons — the classic group is dic, duc, fac, fer
hints.scribe (write), lege (read), curre (run): These have regular imperative forms — the shortened group is dic, duc, fac, fer

hints.similarly enough: 'Similiter' does not imply 'enough' — it simply means 'likewise' or 'in the same way'
hints.at the same time: 'At the same time' is 'simul' — 'similiter' means 'in the same way'
